operating_state: Total net sales increased 2% to $119.6 billion driven by iPhone growth of 6% and Services expansion of 11%, partially offset by iPad declining 25% and Wearables down 11%. Regional performance showed strength in Europe and Japan with double-digit growth while Greater China contracted due to iPhone weakness and currency headwinds. The quarter included 13 weeks versus 14 weeks in the prior year period, creating structural headwind to growth comparisons.
cost_structure: Total gross margin expanded to 45.9% from 43.0% driven by Products margin improvement to 39.4% from cost savings and favorable mix, while Services margin increased to 72.8% from mix benefits. Operating expenses remained flat at 12% of sales with R&D unchanged and selling expenses up modestly. The company noted ongoing volatility and downward pressure expectations for future gross margins due to various market factors.
material_events: The company repurchased $20.5 billion in common stock and paid $3.8 billion in dividends during the quarter. Apple announced updated MacBook Pro and iMac models. In January 2024, the company announced significant changes to iOS, App Store and Safari in the European Union to comply with the Digital Markets Act, including new business terms and alternative distribution methods.
macro_exposures: Currency fluctuations created mixed regional impacts with foreign currency strength benefiting Americas and Europe while renminbi and yen weakness hurt Greater China and Japan. The company faces intensified regulatory scrutiny globally with ongoing antitrust investigations in multiple jurisdictions and potential challenges to its European Union DMA compliance plan. Manufacturing purchase obligations totaled $38.0 billion with substantially all payable within 12 months.
forward_outlook: The company maintains confidence in cash generation and debt market access to meet requirements and capital return programs. Apple intends to increase its quarterly dividend annually subject to board approval, currently at $0.24 per share. Management expects continued business changes in response to regulatory requirements and market conditions across multiple jurisdictions.
uncertainty_profile: Antitrust investigations across multiple jurisdictions could result in significant fines and required business model changes, particularly affecting App Store operations and commercial partnerships. The company's DMA compliance plan remains subject to potential European Commission or private litigation challenges. New accounting standards will be adopted requiring enhanced disclosure requirements for segment reporting and income tax disclosures.
